author: Sangharakshita
title: Contents of Precious Garland - Unchecked
content:
POOR QUALITY ORIGINAL/SCAN IN PLACES
SANGHARAKSHITA IN SEMINAR:

"THE PRECIOUS GARLAND"
CONTENTS
verse
no:
seminar page;
INTRODUCTION
Views on authenticity of Mahayana sutras
Studying with a guru
CH:ONE  HIGH STATUS AND DEFINITE GOODNESS "all knowing"
proving the veracity of Buddhism with science.
intellectualism  Dhammamusari/Sravamusari
The beauty and fascination of of virtues
The sole friend of all beings
Planting the seeds of Dharma/receptivity/generating
Dharma
High Status and Definite goodness
The 'healthy person", receptivity
Happiness and Liberation
Faith and Wisdom
Impotance of Positive emotion;faith,trust,joy.
Investigation of mental states and spontaneity
Pursuing happiness of self and others.
/Ten skilfull actionsPanatipita/not harming others
Adinnadananot taking the not given
Kamesu Micchachara  celibacy -"needs"
and aesthetic appreciation
and enjoyment
and happiness
and warm emotions  homosexuality
False Views  Nihilism/Eternalism
Medicinal drinking
Good livelihood
Honouring the honourable
Self mortification

Consequences of unskilful action
Labelling people
Selfhate, paranoia
Jungian classification;character types; intraverted feeling
Wrong view
Giving and recieving
Wrong livelihood
consequences of arrogance, jealousy
/The Indian 'negative'
The consequences of virtue
The consequences of meditation
the profundity of Nirvana
/Doctrines of definate Goodness  The nonreality of "ego" 
The expansiveness of consciousness
The Hinayanists fears  Rebirth
/The nonterminality of Nirvana
Relativity of terms" cause and effect"
The metaphor of the mirage
The followers of "existance" and "nonexistance"
Buddhism as negation ? 
Nagarjuna' s "approach"
Momentaryness (time) 
Space 
Nonescape from suffering;Relativity of Nirvana/samara 
Fixing "bases"
The non-reality of self
Fear of change;faith in reason


Chapter Two:An Interwoven Explanation of Definite 


Goodness/High Status
lCurrent state of scientific thought on the Universe
Where do souls come from
The illusory elephant
keeping silent
"indigestion" from teachings
Using the teaching properely
Worldly benefits of dana,sila,shanti
Sanghravastus
The benefits of honesty/dishonestyTelling the truth
Speaking with love
Impermanence
Intoxicants,Gambling
Lust for women
Shubha
" "
Violence and entertainment
inspiraton:metta, mudita ,Karuna,Uppeksa
294302Function of art
Great Men, and the  Marks  Beauty
Religuaries
Representation of Beauty
The character of the Buddha
Friendship and sex


Chapter Three: The Collections for Enlightenment


Principal Stages of the Spiritual Path
Trikaya
laziness
The Bodhisattva; practibility of a limitless ideal
making vows
Desire
Building 'images'of people
Suffering
Enlightenment; far or near?
Drawbacks of rich ornamentation,patronage of monks
Importance of metta, emotional positivity
Liberation of Mind(Heart)/ Wisdom
Discipline and Attachments; making vows
Importance of receptivity, sympathy
Meditation diary
Hearing and Giving the Dharma


Chapter Four: Royal Policy


Buddhafields
Accumulating merit
Fame and Flattery  Karma and wealth
Basis of social political frameworks
Buddhist Kings
Pleasure
Boredom
 Meditating “out of time”
The senses and the sense objects
 Pleasure and Pain
 Regression to Infinity
 The Bodhisattva hovering between being and nonbeing
 Did the Buddha teach the Mahayana
 Recognition of the Higher Ideal
Projection
 Impotance of empathy
 of remembering
Giving up pleasures
Hinayana and Mahayana Teachings
 "Extras and Essentials"
 The teaching of the Hidden intention
 Monk and layman; personal development

Chapter Five The Bodhisattva Deeds:

Regular Steps
Anger, hiding faults, resentment
Dishonesty,dissimulation, jealousy,miserliness
Hiri and Otopa
Arrogance, comparing oneself with others, hierachy 
The Seven Prides
Reciting faults
Facing up to big faults
Libidinous praise
Holier than thou
Seeking Advice
Loving attatchments and parents
Social pecking order
Work
Hinayana and Mahayana Paths
Is Buddhism selfish ?  Technology and Progress
Ten Bhumis
"seven fold Puja"
Order Metta
Reliance on contact with wise men
Lamas and Reincarnat Bodhisattvas
Archaryas
General discussion
end...

Song of Four Mindfulnesses:

Introduction
Verse one
esoteric apsects of the Refuges
Verse two
Verse three
Visualisation
Verse four
Verse five
Conclusion
ascertaining spiritual attainment
Sunyata practices
General discussion

FINIS
